corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: Here we propose a mechanism to regularize the classifier layer by estimating the marginalized effect of label-dropout during training.
We propose a mechanism for encouraging the model to be less confident.

We compare our numbers with the best published single-model inference results on the ILSVRC 2012 classification benchmark. the fully connected layer of the auxiliary classifier is also batch-normalized, not just the convolutions.
We propose to use the prior distribution over labels as u(k).

We benchmark our methods on the ILSVRC 2012 classification challenge validation set demonstrate substantial gains over the state of the art: 21.2% top-1 and 5.6% top-5 error for single frame evaluation using a network with a computational cost of 5 billion multiply-adds per inference and with using less than 25 million parameters.

We benchmark our methods on the ILSVRC 2012 classification challenge validation set demonstrate substantial gains over the state of the art: 21.2% top-1 and 5.6% top-5 error for single frame evaluation using a network with a computational cost of 5 billion multiply-adds per inference and with using less than 25 million parameters.

We benchmark our methods on the ILSVRC 2012 classification challenge validation set demonstrate substantial gains over the state of the art: 21.2% top-1 and 5.6% top-5 error for single frame evaluation using a network with a computational cost of 5 billion multiply-adds per inference and with using less than 25 million parameters.
VGGNet and GoogLeNet yielded simi-larly high performance in the 2014 ILSVRC classification challenge.

This means that architectural improvements in deep convolutional architecture can be utilized for improving performance for most other computer vision tasks that are increasingly reliant on high quality, learned visual features.
On the other hand, the Inception architecture of GoogLeNet was also designed to perform well even under strict constraints on memory and computational budget.
Furthermore, these methods could be applied to optimize the Inception architecture as well, widening the efficiency gap again.
Still, the complexity of the Inception architecture makes it more difficult to make changes to the network.
Still, the complexity of the Inception architecture makes it more difficult to make changes to the network.
If the architecture is scaled up naively, large parts of the computational gains can be immediately lost.
Also, does not provide a clear description about the contributing factors that lead to the various design decisions of the GoogLeNet architecture.
Also, does not provide a clear description about the contributing factors that lead to the various design decisions of the GoogLeNet architecture.
Note that LSR achieves the desired goal of preventing the largest logit from becoming much larger than all others.
Table shows the experimental results about the recognition performance of our proposed architecture (Inception-v2) as described in Section 6.
We have provided several design principles to scale up convolutional networks and studied them in the context of the Inception architecture.
We have provided several design principles to scale up convolutional networks and studied them in the context of the Inception architecture.
Our highest quality version of Inception-v3 reaches 21.2%, top-1 and 5.6% top-5 error for single crop evaluation on the ILSVR 2012 classification, setting a new state of the art.

Still our solution uses much less computation than the best published results based on denser networks: our model outperforms the results of He et al -cutting the top-5 (top-1) error by 25% (14%) relative, respectively -while being six times cheaper computationally and using at least five times less parameters (estimated).

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
